name: Bomi County (Liberia)
description: Purple field with the Liberian national flag as a canton in the upper hoist, a large pale-blue disc in the fly half representing the sun, and a brown mountain landscape across the lower fly representing the Bomi iron-ore hills.
history: Presented by President William V. S. Tubman on 29 November 1965 together with the flags of the other Liberian counties.

features.symbolism: Bomi as part of the Republic of Liberia
the dawn of progress over Bomi County
the Bomi Hills, source of the iron ore that historically anchored the county's economy
